name: Phase 2a: GKR
summary:
This phase runs the fractional / LogUp sumcheck via layered GKR. Starting from a root denominator claim q0_claim, the verifier reduces through K = n_logup + l_skip layers, each layer executing a cubic sumcheck of length layer_idx and linearly interpolating the next-layer claim at a fresh challenge mu. The final output is a single evaluation point xi and a reduced (numer, denom) claim that is handed to BatchConstraint.

Four AIRs collaborate. GkrInputAir owns per-proof bookkeeping: PoW grinding, alpha sampling, observing the root claim, and dispatching to the layer chain. GkrLayerAir handles per-layer logic (root zero-check, layer-claim observation, mu sampling, handoff between layers). GkrLayerSumcheckAir runs the cubic sumcheck rounds inside each non-root layer. GkrXiSamplerAir samples any extra xi coordinates when the zerocheck dimension n_max exceeds the LogUp dimension n_logup. The resulting xi vector is assembled across three AIRs on XiRandomnessBus.

blocks.html: The preamble has already produced a GkrModuleMessage per proof carrying (tidx, n_logup, n_max, is_n_max_greater). GKR picks that message up, runs the reduction, and emits a BatchConstraintModuleMessage carrying the reduced claim and the post-GKR transcript cursor. Everything in between is a schedule of transcript observations, samples, and a chain of layer-wise sumchecks — all wired through permutation buses so that imbalance is the only way a prover can cheat.

blocks.heading: GkrInputAir
blocks.html: GkrInputAir is the one-row-per-proof driver. It receives the handoff from the preamble, runs LogUp proof-of-work, samples alpha_logup, observes the root denominator claim q0_claim, and bookkeeps transcript-index arithmetic for the entire GKR schedule. It then dispatches the layer chain and the optional xi-sampler, receives their outputs, and hands the reduced claim to BatchConstraint.

blocks.heading: GkrInputCols
blocks.explain: One row per proof. The row carries the shape counts (n_logup, n_max), the starting transcript index tidx, the root q0_claim (EF-valued), the sampled alpha_logup (EF-valued), the final reduced input_layer_claim coming back from the layer chain, and the PoW witness/sample pair. The IsZero aux cells let the AIR decide whether n_logup == 0 (i.e. the proof has no interactions at all).

blocks.heading: Boolean + proof-index + is-zero sub-AIRs
blocks.explain: Before touching transcript or layer buses, boilerplate fires: is_n_max_greater_than_n_logup is forced boolean, the proof index is enforced to increment by one per enabled row, and IsZeroSubAir sets is_n_logup_zero to the indicator of n_logup == 0. The latter becomes the main case split below: proofs with no LogUp interactions still need xi coordinates but skip the entire layer chain.

blocks.heading: Degenerate case: n_logup == 0
blocks.explain: When the proof has no interactions, the GKR chain is skipped entirely. The AIR nevertheless hands BatchConstraint a well-defined claim: input_layer_claim = [0, alpha_logup]. Downstream, BC subtracts alpha_logup from the denominator, leaving [0, 0] — a trivially satisfied LogUp identity. This lets the BC pipeline be dimension-generic regardless of whether the proof actually has interactions.

blocks.heading: Transcript-index arithmetic
blocks.explain: The schedule's transcript footprint is computed as a linear combination of shape counts. logup_pow_offset is 0 or 2 (one obs + one sample). Alpha and beta together occupy 2 * D_EF slots. Each GKR layer j contributes j sumcheck rounds (4 EF slots each) plus 4 EF claim observations, giving the closed form K * (K + 2) * 2 * D_EF where K = num_layers. If n_max > n_logup or there are no interactions, extra xi slots are appended. tidx_end is the cursor passed to BatchConstraint.

blocks.heading: Dispatch to GkrLayerAir and GkrXiSamplerAir
blocks.explain: Two internal chains are threaded from here. The layer chain starts at the transcript slot right after q0_claim is observed (offset by +D_EF), and a matching GkrLayerOutputMessage is expected back carrying the final input_layer_claim along with layer_idx_end = num_layers - 1. The xi sampler is entered at index num_layers (or 0 if there are no interactions) and must exit at index n_max + l_skip - 1. Because these are permutation buses, any miscount on either side causes imbalance.

blocks.heading: Receive preamble handoff (GkrModuleBus)
blocks.explain: This is the receive side of the preamble handoff: the preamble already committed to (tidx, n_logup, n_max, is_n_max_greater) for each proof on GkrModuleBus, and this AIR picks those values up. Because the bus is a permutation, the row's shape counts are forced to equal whatever the preamble sent — the prover can't mis-declare the LogUp dimensions here.

blocks.heading: PoW observation + sample
blocks.explain: If logup_pow_bits > 0, the AIR first observes the prover's PoW witness at tidx and then samples a base-field challenge at tidx + 1. These are ordinary observe/sample calls (base-field, single slot each) on the global TranscriptBus. The constraint that this sample satisfies the grinding difficulty is enforced by a separate ExpBitsLenBus lookup below.

blocks.heading: Sample alpha_logup (and reserve beta slot)
blocks.explain: alpha_logup is sampled as an EF element at tidx + logup_pow_offset. The next D_EF slots are deliberately left untouched here: BatchConstraint's InteractionsFoldingAir will sample beta_logup at tidx + logup_pow_offset + D_EF via the InteractionsFoldingInputBus handoff below. Because TranscriptBus enforces per-slot ownership, only one AIR can touch any given tidx, so the alpha/beta interleave is safe.

blocks.heading: Observe q0_claim
blocks.explain: At tidx + logup_pow_offset + 2*D_EF the root denominator claim q0_claim is observed as an EF element — but only when has_interactions; if n_logup == 0 no transcript slot is consumed and no claim exists. That same value is re-checked at the root-layer boundary inside GkrLayerAir via the root consistency constraint q_cross == q0_claim, so the observed value is tied both to the sponge state and to the algebraic structure of the first GKR layer.

blocks.heading: Handoff to BatchConstraint
blocks.explain: Three messages are shipped out. BatchConstraintModuleBus carries the final reduced claim [numer, denom - alpha_logup] — the alpha subtraction normalizes the running LogUp accumulator so BC sees a clean zero-sum target. ConstraintsFoldingInputBus hands BC the transcript cursor tidx_end for lambda sampling. InteractionsFoldingInputBus points BC at the reserved beta_logup slot inside GKR's transcript range.

blocks.heading: PoW difficulty lookup
blocks.explain: Finally, the grinding check. ExpBitsLenBus is a global lookup table that maps (base, bit_src, num_bits) -> result, where result = 1 iff bit_src has num_bits low zero bits (modelled as base^{bit_src} for base = GENERATOR). Matching a row with result = 1 is equivalent to asserting the PoW condition — and because bit_src equals the sampled logup_pow_sample, the prover must have searched for a witness that forces the sponge into a state whose low pow_bits are zero.

blocks.heading: GkrLayerAir
blocks.html: GkrLayerAir has one row per (proof, layer). The root layer enforces the LogUp boundary identities p_cross = 0 and q_cross = q0_claim; every layer observes (p_xi_0, q_xi_0, p_xi_1, q_xi_1), samples mu, and linearly interpolates the next-layer claim. Non-root layers additionally sample lambda for batching numer/denom and dispatch a sumcheck to GkrLayerSumcheckAir.

blocks.heading: GkrLayerCols
blocks.explain: Each row holds one layer's entire state: the layer index, the incoming sumcheck_claim_in, four layer-claim EF elements, the sampled lambda and mu, the reduced (numer_claim, denom_claim), and the eq_at_r_prime value returned from the sumcheck AIR. is_first marks the root of each proof's layer chain, is_dummy tags a singleton placeholder row for proofs with no interactions.

blocks.heading: Layer-index loop counter
blocks.explain: The NestedForLoopSubAir<1> wires the shared (proof_idx, is_first) structure: rows within a proof are contiguous and in order. Then layer_idx is pinned to 0 on is_first and to prev + 1 on transitions. The layer chain cannot skip or repeat a layer.

blocks.heading: Dummy-row structural constraints
blocks.explain: A proof with no LogUp interactions contributes exactly one dummy row so that the GkrLayerInputBus permutation still balances with GkrInputAir's send. These constraints force such rows to be true singletons (first == last) and forbid mixing dummy/non-dummy rows within a proof.

blocks.heading: Root-layer zero-check
blocks.explain: The defining LogUp identity at the root: p_cross = p_xi_0 * q_xi_1 + p_xi_1 * q_xi_0 = 0 (numerator sums telescope to zero) and q_cross = q_xi_0 * q_xi_1 = q0_claim (the observed root denominator). Here sumcheck_claim_in at the root row is forced by GkrLayerInputBus to equal q0_claim from GkrInputAir, so the consistency check ties the claim to the transcript.

blocks.heading: Reduce to single evaluation (numer/denom at mu)
blocks.explain: Every layer produces a single (numer, denom) pair by linearly interpolating the two-point evaluations at mu. This is the “reduce from a 2-point claim to a 1-point claim” step of GKR. Both numer_claim and denom_claim are materialized as explicit columns so downstream rows can reference them without recomputation.

blocks.heading: Layer-to-layer RLC handoff
blocks.explain: Between layers, the incoming claim for the next layer is numer + lambda_next * denom — a fresh random-linear combination. The lambda is sampled on the next row (non-root), so the value used in the combination is pinned to a specific transcript slot. The tidx arithmetic accounts for: the optional lambda sample (D_EF), the sumcheck rounds (layer_idx * 4 * D_EF), and the 5 EF slots for observing the four layer claims plus sampling mu.

blocks.heading: GkrLayerInputBus receive (root) + GkrLayerOutputBus send (last)
blocks.explain: The root-layer row pulls (tidx, q0_claim) off GkrLayerInputBus, matching GkrInputAir's send. The last-layer row pushes (tidx_end, layer_idx_end, [numer_claim, denom_claim]) onto GkrLayerOutputBus, completing the chain. GkrInputAir's layer_idx_end = num_layers - 1 receive pins the chain length exactly.

blocks.heading: Dispatch sumcheck (non-root layers)
blocks.explain: Each non-root layer dispatches a sumcheck: it sends (layer_idx, is_last_layer, tidx+D_EF, claim_in) to the sumcheck AIR and receives back (tidx_after_sumcheck, claim_out, eq_at_r_prime). The received claim_out is algebraically forced to equal (p_cross + lambda * q_cross) * eq_at_r_prime — the layer consistency identity. The GkrSumcheckChallengeBus send seeds the sumcheck's first round with mu (the reduction point of the previous layer).

blocks.heading: Sample lambda + observe layer claims + sample mu
blocks.explain: On non-root layers, lambda is sampled at tidx. Then (on every real layer) the four EF-valued layer claims are observed contiguously starting at tidx_after_sumcheck, and mu is sampled at the slot right after. This matches the schedule tallied in GkrInputAir's tidx arithmetic: each layer j uses 4 * j * D_EF slots for its sumcheck (handled by the sumcheck AIR) plus 5 * D_EF slots here.

blocks.heading: Emit xi[0] = mu on last layer
blocks.explain: On the final layer row, mu is published as xi[0] on XiRandomnessBus. This is the first entry of the final evaluation point the GKR reduction commits to; the remaining entries come from the sumcheck's last-layer challenges and the xi-sampler.

blocks.heading: GkrLayerSumcheckAir
blocks.html: GkrLayerSumcheckAir has one row per sumcheck sub-round. For each non-root layer j, it runs j cubic sumcheck rounds: observe (ev1, ev2, ev3), sample r_i, assert claim_out = cubic_interp(ev0, ev1, ev2, ev3)(r_i) where ev0 = claim_in - ev1, and incrementally update the eq polynomial. On the last layer it additionally emits each sampled r_i on XiRandomnessBus at index round + 1.

blocks.heading: GkrLayerSumcheckCols
blocks.explain: Each row is one sub-round. It holds the three EF evaluations ev1, ev2, ev3 (the prover's cubic polynomial's values at 1, 2, 3), the claim flowing through the round, prev_challenge (the corresponding coordinate of the previous layer's xi point) and the freshly sampled challenge, plus the eq polynomial's running value eq_in / eq_out.

blocks.heading: Nested loop: (proof_idx, round within layer_idx)
blocks.explain: A NestedForLoopSubAir<2> enforces the (proof_idx, layer_idx - 1) nested counter structure. The round column starts at 0 on is_first_round, increments by 1 per sub-round, and is forced to reach exactly layer_idx - 1 on the last round — so layer j runs exactly j rounds (the triangular GKR sumcheck shape).

blocks.heading: Eq initialization + update
blocks.explain: At the first round of a layer the eq product starts at 1 (EF). Each round multiplies eq by the standard Lagrange factor xi*ri + (1-xi)*(1-ri), where xi = prev_challenge (the corresponding coordinate of the previous layer's evaluation point) and ri = challenge (the freshly sampled round challenge). The running product is propagated across rows via eq_out -> next.eq_in.

blocks.heading: Cubic interpolation of the round claim
blocks.explain: The “sum constraint” s(0) + s(1) = claim_in is folded directly into ev0 := claim_in - ev1, so only three evaluations are observed. Then claim_out = s(challenge) via cubic Lagrange interpolation through (0, ev0), (1, ev1), (2, ev2), (3, ev3). The outgoing claim is propagated to the next round's claim_in, and tidx advances by 4 * D_EF (three observations plus one sample) per round.

blocks.heading: Sumcheck dispatch buses
blocks.explain: The first-round row receives GkrSumcheckInputMessage (dispatched by GkrLayerAir) — claim_in is pinned to GkrLayerAir's sumcheck_claim_in. The last-round row sends back GkrSumcheckOutputMessage carrying the final claim_out and the fully-accumulated eq_at_r_prime = eq_out. GkrLayerAir's algebraic check claim_out == (p_cross + lambda * q_cross) * eq_at_r_prime closes the loop.

blocks.heading: Previous-layer challenges on GkrSumcheckChallengeBus
blocks.explain: The eq update needs the corresponding coordinate xi_round of the previous layer's final evaluation point. Each round receives (layer_idx - 1, round, prev_challenge) off GkrSumcheckChallengeBus, matched with a send from the previous layer's sumcheck (or the mu send from GkrLayerAir at round 0). Each round also sends its freshly-sampled challenge forward for the next layer's consumption — except on the last layer, whose challenges leave via XiRandomnessBus instead.

blocks.heading: Sumcheck transcript + last-layer xi emission
blocks.explain: Per sub-round: observe three EF evaluations, sample one EF challenge — exactly 4 * D_EF transcript slots. On the last layer (is_last_layer = 1), each sampled challenge is additionally emitted on XiRandomnessBus at index round + 1, building up xi[1..num_layers]. Combined with GkrLayerAir's xi[0] = mu and GkrXiSamplerAir's extras, this fully populates the evaluation point.

blocks.heading: GkrXiSamplerAir
blocks.html: GkrXiSamplerAir generates the tail of the xi vector when the zerocheck dimension n_max strictly exceeds the GKR dimension n_logup (or when there are no interactions at all). It is a thin loop: one row per extra coordinate, each row samples one EF value from the transcript and publishes it on XiRandomnessBus at the correct index.

blocks.heading: GkrXiSamplerCols
blocks.explain: One row per additional xi coordinate. The row carries the challenge idx, the sampled EF value xi, and the transcript slot tidx it was sampled at. is_first_challenge marks the first row of each proof's run.

blocks.heading: Loop counter + tidx increment
blocks.explain: Standard loop wiring: idx increments by 1 per row, tidx increments by D_EF. Dummy rows (used when this AIR is entered but produces no coordinates) must be singletons. The starting (idx, tidx) and the ending (idx, tidx) are both pinned by GkrXiSamplerBus via the matching sends and receives in GkrInputAir.

blocks.heading: XiSampler dispatch handoff
blocks.explain: The first-challenge row receives the starting (idx, tidx) from GkrInputAir on GkrXiSamplerBus (the latter sent idx = has_interactions * num_layers, which is num_layers when there are interactions or 0 when not). The last-challenge row sends back the ending (idx, tidx_end), with the idx matching GkrInputAir's expected n_max + l_skip - 1 and tidx_end being tidx of the last sample plus one D_EF slot — jointly forcing the exact count of xi coordinates produced.

blocks.heading: Sample xi + publish on XiRandomnessBus
blocks.explain: Each non-dummy row samples one EF challenge on TranscriptBus at tidx and publishes it on XiRandomnessBus at the current idx. Combined with GkrLayerAir's idx = 0 message and GkrLayerSumcheckAir's idx = 1..num_layers messages, the receivers in BatchConstraint and Stacking (EqNsAir, EqSharpUniAir, etc.) see a complete, index-addressable xi vector of length n_max + l_skip.

blocks.html: At the end of the phase, GkrInputAir's BatchConstraintModuleBus send delivers the reduced claim [numer, denom - alpha_logup] and the post-GKR transcript cursor tidx_bc = tidx_end + D_EF to the Batch Constraint phase. The xi vector has been assembled in parallel on XiRandomnessBus, ready to be consumed by BC's eq-polynomial AIRs and the Stacking phase's WHIR point. Every transcript slot touched is deterministic in the shape counts, every claim flowing between AIRs rides a permutation bus, and every sampled challenge is pinned to the Poseidon2 sponge — so the prover has no freedom beyond the values it commits to in the extracted proof.
